CSCO.rationale: CSCO trades at a depressed 14.2x P/E with 22.9% net margins, 29.7% ROE, and a 3.5% dividend yield, but revenue growth has stalled (FY22 +3.5% YoY) and the stock is in a clear downtrend (-27.5% YoY) with analyst price targets being cut, making this a mild long leaning on valuation rather than momentum.
key_factors: P/E of 14.2x is at the low end of 1-year range (14.2-23.8x)
Strong profitability: net margin 22.9%, ROE 29.7%, gross margin 62.5%
Revenue growth stalled: FY22 $51.56B vs prior year, with quarterly revenue flat at ~$12.7-13.1B
Stock down -27.5% over 1 year and -4.7% over 3 months, in clear downtrend
HC Wainwright cut price target on CSCO on 2022-09-22, signaling analyst caution
Low debt-to-equity of 0.224 provides balance sheet flexibility

sources_cited.excerpt: HC Wainwright Cuts Price Target On This Stock By 50%? Also This Analyst Slashes PT On Cisco

WMT.rationale: WMT trades at a deep-value 8.5x PE (bottom of its 8.1-16.4 1-year range) with a respectable 16.4% ROE, offering attractive risk-adjusted reward, but razor-thin 2.4% net margins cap conviction given ongoing inflation/cost pressure.
key_factors: PE diluted 8.47x at the very bottom of the 8.1-16.4 1-year range — extreme relative cheapness
ROE 16.4% with P/B 1.39x — capital efficiency supports the multiple floor
Q2 FY23 EPS recovered to $1.88 from a weak Q1 $0.74, showing earnings resilience
Net margin only 2.4% / operating margin 4.1% — leaves little buffer for further cost shocks
1-year total return -5.4% vs. a brutal bear market — defensive characteristics intact
